task_completion.description: Agent opens the 'Braille Alphabets' activity in GCompris.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the GCompris application running with the 'Braille Alphabets' activity open, identifiable by a 6-dot interactive Braille cell and a target letter.

task_completion.description: Agent successfully completes the first Braille letter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the correct dots selected for the first target letter, accompanied by UI feedback such as a progress indicator advancing, a success animation, or transitioning to a new letter.

task_completion.description: Agent successfully completes a second, different Braille letter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the agent completing a second distinct target letter, with the activity's progress indicator showing two completed items.

task_completion.description: Agent successfully completes a third, different Braille letter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the agent completing a third distinct target letter, with the activity's progress indicator showing at least three completed items.

task_completion.description: Agent returns to the GCompris main menu after completing the letters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the GCompris main menu screen (displaying the various activity categories) after the Braille activity has been exited.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted exclusively with the GCompris graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show cursor movements, clicks, and GUI focus within the GCompris application window. There should be no terminal windows executing commands to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: Verify that the completion of letters is genuine and sequential.
integrity.visual_evidence: The target letters change naturally and the in-game progress indicators update sequentially. The agent should not be using developer tools or memory editing to force the progress bar to advance.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use external scripts or text editors to simulate completion.
integrity.visual_evidence: Only the GCompris application and standard OS UI are visible during the task execution. No scripts, automated testing frameworks, or terminal windows are used to manipulate the game state.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The verified activity name is 'braille_alphabets'. It teaches Braille using a 6-dot interactive cell where the left column contains dots 1, 2, 3 (top to bottom) and the right column contains dots 4, 5, 6 (top to bottom). Note: Although the task description instructs the agent to look in the 'Discovery' category, the activity is officially located in the 'other' (miscellaneous) category in GCompris.
